title: Stress-test CV with chronology gaps, then keep only ensemble members that survive
summary: This dataset mixes years and repeated races/drivers, so leakage-sensitive features can look strong under naive grouped CV.
rationale: This dataset mixes years and repeated races/drivers, so leakage-sensitive features can look strong under naive grouped CV. Time-aware docs and survival literature both emphasize evaluating on later periods only; for Kaggle, robust public-LB behavior often comes from pruning models that collapse under stricter splits and calibrating only on unbiased OOF predictions.
implementation_hint: Run two validation views: `GroupKFold` by `Race_Year` and forward-only `TimeSeriesSplit` over season order with a gap. Keep models/features whose fold variance stays low across both. Fit final sigmoid/isotonic calibrators on stacked OOF predictions only, and average only stable members.
expected_effect: Less optimistic local CV, better leaderboard transfer, and cleaner probabilities from a small stable ensemble rather than a single overfit booster.
risk: This may lower local CV while improving real generalization; isotonic can overfit if each calibration slice is too small.
